strengths: Demonstrated good instrument handling and coordination throughout the attempt
Maintained steady camera positioning and workspace organization
Successfully completed multiple object transfers showing basic laparoscopic skills
weaknesses: Failed to identify the correct task setup - performed peg transfer instead of precision cutting
Did not recognize or utilize the gauze material required for Task 2
Missed the fundamental task objective of cutting a marked circle
Exceeded the 300-second time limit without task completion

drills.name: Task identification rehearsal
drills.description: Practice identifying Task 2 setup by locating the gauze with printed circle and positioning Maryland grasper for traction before any cutting begins.
drills.targets_weakness: Failed to identify the correct task setup.

drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining traction with the Maryland grasper to improve scissor entry angle.
drills.targets_weakness: Missed the fundamental task objective of cutting a marked circle.

drills.name: Traction-angle practice
drills.description: 10 repetitions of positioning the Maryland grasper to provide optimal traction while the scissors approach the cutting line.
drills.targets_weakness: Did not recognize or utilize the gauze material required for Task 2.

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on task recognition before instrument manipulation. Review the FLS Task 2 setup requirements and practice identifying the correct materials before attempting the cutting sequence.
